episode_text:
day=2; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Personal; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item in a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed fruit meal was explicitly selected to be served without reheating. IF food is unspecified/non-fruit in a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral THEN 1 min BECAUSE earlier evidence supported a mild reheating default and no broader context-dependent variation has been observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a device positioned to the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility of the laptop on the right. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly accepted no special occlusion handling in that context.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a slower robot pace in that personal meal. IF dining context is task-focused, such as working on a laptop on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN fast BECAUSE the user previously chose faster operation in that context, consistent with prioritizing efficiency.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition in a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this option has been selected consistently and no context-dependent variation has been observed.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN no BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to skip confirmation in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN no BECAUSE the user also preferred to avoid this extra interaction step there. Overall, the user currently favors reduced interaction burden over explicit bite-acquisition confirmation.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is physically incompatible with the user's abilities.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer, which is not compatible with the user's physical abilities.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly selected multimodal readiness cues in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously selected speech-only cues there.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user has severe upper-limb paralysis and cannot press physical buttons. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly selected this low-effort method and has good mouth/head control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred automatic initiation in that task-focused context.

IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user has severe upper-limb paralysis and cannot press physical buttons. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly selected this low-effort method and has good mouth/head control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred automatic initiation in that task-focused context.

IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user has severe upper-limb paralysis and cannot press physical buttons. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected automatic initiation for mouth wiping in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected it there. Overall, mouth-wiping initiation currently appears to favor automation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly selected multimodal completion cues in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously selected speech-only completion cues there.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. IF feeding transfer completion can be detected by robot perception THEN perception BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected this reliable, low-effort option across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. IF drinking transfer completion can be detected by robot perception using head gestures THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head/neck control and has repeatedly selected this low-effort option across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF physical button use is required THEN do not use button BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. IF mouth-wiping completion can be detected by robot perception using head gestures THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head/neck control and has repeatedly selected this low-effort option across contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN no BECAUSE the user explicitly accepted faster operation without retracting in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN no BECAUSE the user also accepted not retracting there, despite some concern about right-side visibility. Overall, the user currently prioritizes efficiency over retracting between tasks.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce such as ketchup AND the user explicitly chooses not to use it in the current context THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal included ketchup and the user still preferred no dipping. IF food is fruit or otherwise non-dippable in a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly selected no dipping in that context as well.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food is fruit/non-dippable THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a longer wait in that context. IF the user is using autocontinue for transfer initiation in a task-focused context such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is unspecified/non-dippable THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a short wait that reduces interaction time there.
